$comment: Vendor-wide Configuration blob for the YourMembership Integration row. Targets the `upsert_integration_configuration` MCP tool documented in .claude/agents/metadata-writer.md. Each top-level key is named to match the workflow contract in that agent doc. NOTE: as of v5.39.x-phase0, the MJ: Integrations schema does NOT have a Configuration column — these facts are emitted here as a sidecar so the code-builder downstream has them, and so the runtime D4 SoftPKClassifier has the universalPK hint. When the Integration table gains a Configuration nvarchar(MAX) column (per plans/integration-framework-redesign.md), this file's contents copy verbatim into the Integration row's Configuration field.
universalPKNote: YourMembership has NO vendor-wide PK convention. Different resource families use different PK shapes — ProfileID (int) for Members, MemberID + ClientID composite paths for Member sub-resources, EventID for Events, and per-resource integer IDs elsewhere. The runtime SoftPKClassifier must per-IO classify; there is no single fieldName that holds for all 160 Ams families. Source: walking the OpenAPI spec's path templates for `/Ams/{ClientID}/Member/{MemberID}/...` vs `/Ams/{ClientID}/Event/{EventId}/...` vs `/Ams/{ClientID}/CampaignEmailLists` parameter sets confirms the PK heterogeneity. Recorded explicitly so downstream agents do not invent a default.

Primary.SessionTTLSource: Getting Started with the REST API PDF (2021), §Authentication: 'Session expires after 15 minutes of inactivity'

Legacy.Note: Legacy YM-specific shape (capitalised, AppSecret/AppSecert dual-spelling — vendor's typo deprecation banner). Prefer /OAuth/GetToken for new integrations.

AuthFlowDetail.ClientCredentialsEvidence: /OAuth/GetToken parameter list per OpenAPI: [grant_type, scope, code, refresh_token, redirect_uri, client_id, client_secret]. No client_credentials grant is documented or available; only authorization_code and refresh_token grants are present.

AuthHeaderPattern.Note: CORS preflight reply at every endpoint lists both `X-ss-id` and `Authorization` in Access-Control-Allow-Headers. Either header is accepted depending on which auth flow issued the credential.

PaginationDefaults.Note: 112 of 297 paths declare both PageSize and PageNumber query parameters. A minority of paths also accept OffSet/Offset/limit (5 distinct pagination parameter names observed across the spec) — per-operation pagination is captured in MJ: Integration Objects.PaginationType. The default for IOs that declare both PageSize+PageNumber is PageNumber pagination, 1-indexed. Default page size is per-operation (no global default published).

IncrementalSyncCapability.Note: No vendor-wide incremental-sync mechanism (no /sync endpoint, no Last-Modified-Since query semantic across all resources). Per-resource incremental support, where present, is via per-operation modifiedSince-style query parameters declared on individual paths. Therefore the vendor-level capability is FALSE; per-IO IncrementalSync flags live on MJ: Integration Objects.

WebhooksAvailable.Note: No first-party webhook documentation exists for YM. The OpenAPI surface includes ZoomEventListener and ZoomEventListenerOAuth endpoints (Zoom integration callbacks YM consumes from Zoom), but those are inbound from a third party into YM, not outbound YM-to-customer webhooks. SOURCE_STUDY §5 explicitly records this as a gap. No signature algorithm is documented.

BulkOperationsAvailable.Pattern: Informz bulk-upload family (cross-product, Informz email-marketing sibling product)
BulkOperationsAvailable.Note: These are bulk PUSH endpoints to a sibling Community Brands product (Informz). There is no general-purpose bulk read or batch import API for arbitrary YM resources. Other bulk-shaped endpoints: StoreProductBulkStatus, StoreProductBulkStatusAll, PeopleBulkDetach — each is resource-specific, not a generic batching surface.

APIVersioningStrategy.EvidenceFromOpenAPI: info.version='1.0, ServiceStack: 5.0.2.0' — only one published version; no /v1/, /v2/, no Accept-Version or X-Version header documented in any operation
APIVersioningStrategy.Note: No changelog or version history is published. The base path is `/` and resources sit directly under /Ams/{ClientID}/. ServiceStack runtime version (5.0.2) is exposed via the X-Powered-By response header but is the server framework, not the API contract version.

Session.Note: Session cookies do NOT have a refresh mechanism — when the SessionId expires, the client must call /Ams/Authenticate again with stored credentials.

OAuth2.Note: Refresh-token expiry not documented. Initial token issuance happens via grant_type=authorization_code after the user is redirected through Admin → API Configuration → OAuth Management → Add Client App's configured Client App.

ErrorResponseShape.Note: Captured live from POST /Ams/Authenticate with empty body. ResponseStatus is present on EVERY response — success responses carry an empty ResponseStatus (no ErrorCode), error responses carry a populated one. Connector error-detection should test `body.ResponseStatus?.ErrorCode != null` rather than HTTP status code, because some validation errors return 200 with a populated ResponseStatus.
ErrorResponseShape.ErrorCatalog: Vendor does not publish a closed list of ErrorCode values. Observed values include NotNull, Predicate. The catalog must be built empirically — recorded as a gap.

CustomFieldMarkerPattern.ResponseShape: CustomFieldResponse { FieldCode, Fields, Visibility, VisibilityInt, Values, ValuesProxy, MetaValue, ClientID }
CustomFieldMarkerPattern.Note: YM exposes per-member custom fields via a separate CustomField endpoint family rather than inlining custom fields in the MemberProfile response. The CustomField response uses FieldCode (vendor-assigned) plus Values/ValuesProxy for the field's value list. There is NO marker pattern in field names (no `cf_`, no `custom_`) — the discriminant is the endpoint itself.

ResponseEnvelopeShape.Note: Every /Ams response carries a ServiceStack envelope. Confirmed by inspection of multiple definitions (CampaignEmailListsResponse, DuesRulesResponse, ConnectionCategoryListResponse, EventRegistrantsResponse, MemberGroupsResponse).
ResponseEnvelopeShape.ListPayloadKeyConvention: Per-operation key matches the resource (e.g., CampaignEmailLists, Rules, ConnectionCategories, EventRegistrantsList, GroupTypeList). The ResponseDataKey is per-IO, not vendor-wide.

ContentType.Note: From OpenAPI top-level. Note that auth endpoints accept formData (application/x-www-form-urlencoded) for session and OAuth flows — this is form-encoded post, not JSON. The /Ams data CRUD surface is JSON-only.

RoleGating.Note: Per-operation `Requires any of the roles:` declarations on /metadata pages indicate role-gated operations. Connector callers must use credentials provisioned with the correct role for each operation. Per-IO role requirements should be captured on MJ: Integration Objects when known.

RateLimitPolicy.Evidence: Live HEAD requests to ws.yourmembership.com return no X-RateLimit-* headers, no Retry-After, no quota headers. SOURCE_STUDY §5 confirms vendor publishes no rate-limit documentation. Cloudflare front may apply edge-level protection but does not surface predictable quota info.
RateLimitPolicy.RecommendedClientBehaviour: Honour 429 responses if/when observed; back off on 503; no proactive throttling required by documentation but vendor-side throttling is plausible at high volume.

ClientIDHandling.Source: Site-level identifier issued by YM admin console
ClientIDHandling.Note: ClientID is REQUIRED in essentially every /Ams path. Connector code must inject it from the credential at request-build time. The integer Client ID is also echoed in every response's ResponseStatus envelope, so it round-trips.

AdditionalNotes.ServerVersion: ServiceStack/5.02 NET45 — visible in X-Powered-By header
AdditionalNotes.CORSAllowedHeaders: Content-Type, X-Requested-With, X-ss-id, Accept, Origin, Access-Control-Request-Method, Access-Control-Request-Headers, Authorization
AdditionalNotes.BodyParamCasing: PascalCase in /Ams; snake_case in /OAuth/GetToken (RFC 6749 conformance); PascalCase in /OAuth/GetAccessToken (legacy YM shape)
